novel_features.name: Live pipeline event tail
novel_features.description: Tail Digg's ingestion pipeline in real time — see clusters as they're detected, stories fast-climbing the leaderboard with explicit rank deltas, X posts being processed, batch breakdowns.
novel_features.rationale: Requires reading the public /api/trending/status JSON endpoint that the web UI consumes silently. The web UI shows nothing about pipeline state; this surfaces it.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs 'tell me when story X just climbed N ranks' or 'what new clusters did Digg detect in the last hour', this is the only way.

novel_features.name: Replacement archaeology
novel_features.description: Show stories that were knocked out of the rankings since the last sync, with Digg's own published replacement rationale.
novel_features.rationale: Digg explicitly publishes a replacementRationale field per cluster. The web UI shows current rankings only — once a story drops, the rationale is gone. The local snapshot store keeps it.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Best-of-feed shifts faster than people remember. This makes 'what did Digg drop and why' queryable.

novel_features.name: Score component breakdown
novel_features.description: Print the full ranking transparency record for one cluster — scoreComponents, evidence array, numeratorLabel, percentAboveAverage.
novel_features.rationale: Digg ships gravity-score components and evidence in the page payload. The web UI shows only the final gravity score.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When a user asks 'why is THIS the top story', the answer is structured data; agents can compose with it.

novel_features.name: Time-windowed sentiment
novel_features.description: Read per-time-window positivity ratios (pos6h, pos12h, pos24h, posLast) for a cluster.
novel_features.rationale: Each cluster carries pos6h/12h/24h fields. No web UI surfaces them.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Tells an agent whether the conversation around a story is still net-positive or has soured; useful before quoting a story.

novel_features.name: Cross-aggregator references
novel_features.description: Show this cluster's Hacker News and Techmeme mirrors when Digg has detected the story is being discussed there.
novel_features.rationale: Each cluster carries hackerNews and techmeme cross-reference fields populated by Digg's own pipeline.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Removes the manual 'is HN talking about this too' step from any cross-aggregator research workflow.

novel_features.name: Influence-ranked author leaderboard
novel_features.description: Top accounts in the Digg AI 1000 ranked by Digg's influence score, story count, or reach.
novel_features.rationale: Digg AI's product premise is a ranked 1,000-account leaderboard on X. The /ai/influencers page is currently 404; the data is embedded in every feed response.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Investors and AI scouts care which accounts move the news cycle. Now queryable, sortable, scriptable.

novel_features.name: Rank history per cluster
novel_features.description: Full trajectory of one cluster's currentRank, peakRank, and delta over local snapshot history.
novel_features.rationale: Local snapshots persist what the web UI overwrites every fetch.
novel_features.why_it_matters: 'Entered at #18, peaked at #4 over 6h, dropped to #22 by 24h' is impossible to learn from the live site.

novel_features.name: Author surfacing record
novel_features.description: Every cluster a given X account contributed to, with post type (original, retweet, quote, reply).
novel_features.rationale: Cluster payloads list all contributing X posts with username, xId, displayName, and postType. Local store joins them per author.
novel_features.why_it_matters: 'Show me every story this account surfaced this week' is the investor-scout query.

novel_features.name: Rank-movement watcher
novel_features.description: Poll /ai, diff against last snapshot, alert when any cluster moves N+ ranks.
novel_features.rationale: Read-only polling combined with the local snapshot store and AdaptiveLimiter.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Read-only operational watcher; never writes anything back to Digg.

novel_features.name: Pipeline status dashboard
novel_features.description: One-screen view of /api/trending/status: isFetching, nextFetchAt, storiesToday, clustersToday, last 5 events.
novel_features.rationale: Operational telemetry endpoint exposed publicly; CLI presents it as a dashboard.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets ops and power users see when a fresh batch is about to land and what's been ingested in the last hour.

narrative.headline: Tail the Digg AI 1000's news cycle from the terminal — read-only, with the full pipeline event stream and rank-history nobody else surfaces.
narrative.value_prop: Digg AI is a curated leaderboard of 1,000 AI accounts on X and the story clusters they surface. The web UI shows you today's snapshot. This CLI tails the pipeline events, keeps a local rank-history that survives daily overwrites, and exposes Digg's own replacement rationale and gravity components so an agent can answer 'why this story?' and 'what got dropped overnight?' with structured data.
narrative.auth_narrative: No auth required. The CLI uses only public surfaces — the /ai page (HTML+RSC scrape) and /api/trending/status (public JSON). It does not use Clerk session cookies or any authenticated endpoint, by design: this is a read-only research tool, and Digg's parent platform was shut down over AI-agent abuse. The CLI is read-only and identifies itself with a clear User-Agent so Digg ops can rate-limit it cleanly.

quickstart.comment: Pull the current /ai feed and /api/trending/status events into the local store
Read today's top 10 clusters as structured JSON
See which stories climbed the rankings in the last hour with explicit rank deltas
What got knocked out of the rankings overnight and Digg's own rationale for each
The Digg AI 1000's top influencers by Digg's score

troubleshoots.symptom: All commands return empty after install
troubleshoots.fix: Run `digg-pp-cli sync` first — the local store is empty until the first sync.
The pipeline batches every ~10 minutes. Wait for `nextFetchAt` from `digg-pp-cli pipeline status` or filter by a different `--type`.
Adaptive limiter backs off automatically. If it persists, lower the polling rate with `--interval 120s` on `watch` commands.
Use `clusterUrlId` (the 8-char alphanumeric short ID), not the UUID-style clusterId. `digg-pp-cli top --json --select clusterUrlId` lists them.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent or power user needs structured access to Digg AI's rankings, ranking-change history, pipeline events, or per-cluster transparency record. It is the right tool for tracking AI-news cycle movement, building cross-aggregator research over HN+Techmeme+Digg, or exposing Digg AI signals into a larger automation. Do NOT use it for vote, comment, or post automation — those mutations are explicitly out of scope.

recipes.title: What climbed >=10 ranks in the last hour
recipes.explanation: Reads the public events stream, filters to fast-climb events only, and narrows the JSON to the five fields an agent actually needs.

recipes.title: Why is a story the top story
recipes.explanation: Print the scoreComponents and evidence array for one cluster. Get a clusterUrlId from `digg-pp-cli top --json --select clusterUrlId`.

recipes.title: Show every cluster a given X account contributed to this week
recipes.explanation: Queries the local store for clusters where the named author was a contributor; output is narrowed for agent consumption.

recipes.title: Cross-reference a story across HN and Techmeme
recipes.explanation: Uses Digg's own hackerNews/techmeme reference fields so you don't have to search those sites manually. Pass any clusterUrlId from `top --json --select clusterUrlId`.

recipes.title: Tail the pipeline live
recipes.explanation: One-screen dashboard of isFetching, nextFetchAt, storiesToday, clustersToday, and the last few pipeline events.

trigger_phrases: what climbed the AI news rankings
